description:
You are acting as a Linux systems-engineer who is performing a very small “security hardening” change set.  
Your goal is to:

1. Create one configuration directory and file that emulate a hardened sysctl drop-in.  
2. Append two ulimit commands to the user’s interactive shell start-up file.  
3. Produce a tiny plain-text log so that an automated security auditor can verify what you did.

Required end-state of the system
--------------------------------
1. A new directory must exist:  
   • /home/user/hardening  

2. Inside that directory a single file must exist and contain *exactly* the three lines shown below, in the same order, each terminated by a Unix newline (LF):  
   /home/user/hardening/99-security.conf  

   net.ipv4.conf.all.rp_filter = 1  
   net.ipv4.conf.default.rp_filter = 1  
   kernel.randomize_va_space = 2  

   (The file should have no blank lines and no trailing spaces.)

3. The following two command lines must have been appended (not inserted elsewhere) to the very end of /home/user/.bashrc, each followed by a newline:  

   ulimit -S core 0  
   ulimit -H core 0  

   Hints: make sure you do not accidentally duplicate identical lines if you rerun your commands; appending once is enough for this task.

4. A verification log must exist: /home/user/hardening/hardening_summary.log  
   The log must consist of *exactly* three lines, in this precise key=value format, terminated by a newline after the last line:

   CONFIG_FILE_CREATED=/home/user/hardening/99-security.conf  
   LINES_IN_CONFIG=3  
   BASHRC_UPDATED=yes  

   Do not include any extra whitespace, comments, or blank lines.

Practical guidance
------------------
• All paths are case-sensitive; spell them precisely.  
• You do not have root privileges, so work only inside /home/user.  
• A total of three or four well-chosen shell commands are sufficient to meet all requirements.  
• Once you have completed the steps, double-check the exact contents of both files and the presence of the newline at the end of each line—this is what the automated test will validate.

(This finishes the task description. No direct command examples are provided so you can decide how to achieve the goal.)
truth:
Expected artefacts and their exact contents after successful completion:

1. Directory: /home/user/hardening
   • Must exist and be readable by the regular user.

2. File: /home/user/hardening/99-security.conf  
   Exact byte-for-byte content (including trailing newline at end of final line):
net.ipv4.conf.all.rp_filter = 1\n
net.ipv4.conf.default.rp_filter = 1\n
kernel.randomize_va_space = 2\n

3. File: /home/user/.bashrc
   • Last two physical lines must be:
ulimit -S core 0\n
ulimit -H core 0\n

4. File: /home/user/hardening/hardening_summary.log  
   Exact content:
CONFIG_FILE_CREATED=/home/user/hardening/99-security.conf\n
LINES_IN_CONFIG=3\n
BASHRC_UPDATED=yes\n
name: task_000000_0a968215
